correction.token_correctness_is_the_gate: prior instrumentation used block-0 cache POSITIONS-WRITTEN as the advance/bake proxy. That is UNRELIABLE: block-0's append can write positions while the full-model output is still garbage (151936 after step 1). Under TOKEN correctness (byte-identical to baseline), the full-model RUNTIME_KV decode BAKES regardless of prefill method.
correction.chunked_prefill_real_tokens: T=512x4 chunked prefill with REAL tokens still BAKES (MODE=1 tokens=[13876,151936,151936,...] vs baseline [13876,38835,34208,...]). The earlier 'chunked advances' was a positions-written false positive.
correction.conclusion: The buffer-identity hypothesis is REFUTED: (a) BUFFER_IDENTITY_DIFF_NOT_FOUND (canonical-store-fill and assign-fill have identical uop op/dtype/shape/op_counts; only object ids differ); (b) a full pristine-buffer rebase does NOT fix the token-level baking; (c) chunked/large/full prefill all bake on tokens. The cache buffer is NOT the cause.

recommended_next: Gate on TOKEN correctness (NOT positions-written). Instrument at the GraphRunner kernel level: dump the kv_append CALL's ji_args (the start_pos kernarg value) across eager/capture/replay -- does it actually change per replay? And dump the owned-tile read correctness in-model. The cache/buffer/prefill/rebase angles are exhausted; the remaining question is whether the append's start_pos kernarg (or the owned-tile read) is correct across replays in the full-model GraphRunner.
